title: Gemma Playground: Parallel Agents in Action
summary: Google DeepMind engineer Ian Ballantyne demonstrates Gemma 4 26B running locally with 10 parallel subagents coordinated by an orchestrator. The system processes concurrent tasks autonomously at over 170 tokens per second, generating an SVG art gallery coded simultaneously across agents on a single machine.
writeup: Google DeepMind engineer Ian Ballantyne demonstrates Gemma 4 26B running locally with 10 parallel subagents coordinated by an orchestrator. The demo shows the agents processing concurrent tasks autonomously at over 170 tokens per second, jointly coding an SVG art gallery on a single machine. It matters as a concrete example of the orchestrator pattern and multi-agent batch processing executed entirely on edge hardware rather than in the cloud.
